title: What Time's Throw In? – Irish Sports TV Schedule Web Tool
vision: Provide Irish sports fans with a single, easy-to-use web page that consolidates weekend start times and TV channel/streaming information for all major sports, eliminating the need to check multiple sources.
objectives: Build a single-page, self-contained HTML/CSS/JS web tool that displays upcoming sports start times and their broadcast channels
Cover the top Irish-interest sports: rugby, soccer, GAA (hurling & football), golf, and horse racing
Focus on weekend fixtures as the primary viewing window for fans
Display the TV channel or streaming service for each event clearly
Include clear on-page usage instructions so visitors can immediately understand how to use the tool
Ensure the tool works as a static file served at a path URL with no server-side dependencies

responsibilities: Define sport categories and data structure
Validate the final tool meets the directive
Structure sport schedule data within the static file
Ensure responsive design and cross-browser compatibility
Consume the tool to find sport start times and channels
Provide feedback on usability and completeness

success_criteria: The tool renders correctly as a single static HTML file opened in any modern browser
All five core sports (rugby, soccer, GAA, golf, horse racing) are represented with sample fixture data
Each fixture entry shows: sport, event/match name, date/time, and TV channel or streaming service
Weekend fixtures are prominently displayed or filterable
On-page usage instructions are visible and clear without requiring external documentation
The page is responsive and usable on both desktop and mobile devices
No external database, authentication, or server-side runtime is required
constraints: Must be a single self-contained HTML file (inline CSS and JS, no external dependencies)
No external database or authentication system
Must function as a static file served at a path URL
Data is embedded/static (no live API integrations required in v1.0)

assumptions.assumption: Live data feeds or API integrations are not required for the initial version
assumptions.default: Sport schedule data will be hardcoded as sample/representative fixtures within the HTML file

assumptions.assumption: The tool needs to support all modern browsers but not legacy ones
assumptions.default: Target latest versions of Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge

assumptions.assumption: The scope of 'top sports' is limited to the five listed categories
assumptions.default: Rugby, soccer, GAA (hurling & football), golf, and horse racing

assumptions.assumption: Weekend is defined as Saturday and Sunday
assumptions.default: Fixtures shown default-filter to Saturday and Sunday

assumptions.assumption: No user accounts or personalisation features are needed
assumptions.default: All visitors see the same schedule view with client-side filtering only

assumptions.assumption: Time zone for all displayed times
assumptions.default: All times displayed in Irish Standard Time (IST/GMT)
